                                                     WHAT                                                                             Our Physical Footprint
                                                  Sets Us Apart                                                                       LTF operates a Pan-India distribution network, comprising an extensive branch footprint that enables your Company to serve a
                                                                                                                                      large and diverse customer base across both rural and urban India. Anchored in deep local insights and supported by a scalable
                                                                                                                                      operating model, the network strengthens last-mile connectivity while expanding presence in high-growth urban markets.

Corporate Governance                                                                                                          Governance Philosophy
                                                                                                                              Governance at LTF is designed to evolve in line with changes in the business environment, adapting to shifts in the

GOVERNANCE                                                                                                                    regulatory landscape and stakeholder expectations. This dynamic approach ensures that governance frameworks remain
                                                                                                                              relevant and effective.


THAT BUILDS TRUST                                                                                                             Within this framework, the Board provides strategic guidance to strengthen governance practices and processes across the
                                                                                                                              organisation, while ensuring that ethical conduct is embedded across strategic and operational decisions. It also actively
                                                                                                                              identifies and addresses emerging risks in a timely manner, with regular reviews of policies, processes, and controls further


LTF’s governance framework, rooted in the enduring ethos of the L&T Group,                                                    strengthening institutional resilience. Your Company actively engages with investors, employees, customers, suppliers, and
                                                                                                                              communities, recognising that their trust and support is critical for its success.

provides the stability necessary to pursue long-term growth with unwavering                                                   Your Company integrates the core values of ethics, transparency, accountability and responsible business conduct into its
                                                                                                                              operations. Thereby ensuring strong governance practices, regulatory compliance and transparent disclosures.
discipline. Your Company’s governance framework enables robust oversight,
                                                                                                                              Your Company's governance philosophy serves as the foundation of its decision making, reinforcing its commitment to
transparent disclosures, and effective risk management practices. These                                                       ethical leadership, transparency, and long-term sustainable value creation.
measures ensure responsible operations while consistently safeguarding
stakeholder interests.                                                                                                        Corporate Structure
As your Company accelerates its growth journey, it remains anchored in a risk-first, ethics-led approach that strengthens
resilience, enhances transparency, and reinforces long-term stakeholder trust. By safeguarding stakeholder value while
scaling with discipline, your Company successfully achieved its Lakshya 2026 goals. This milestone lays a strong foundation
for a sustained momentum toward LTF’s Lakshya 2031 vision.

The governance framework ensures regulatory compliance while embedding integrity and responsible operations across all
business functions. It also strengthens oversight mechanisms and integrates strategic foresight into operations. Together,
                                                                                                                                                                                    Larsen & Toubro Limited
                                                                                                                                                                                            (Parent Company)
these elements support sustainable growth while maintaining accountability and organisational discipline.

Governance Structure                                                                                                             Board Committees
Your Company’s governance structure follows a multi-layered framework designed to promote transparency, strengthen               In accordance with the Companies Act, 2013, SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations,
oversight and ensure accountability across all levels of organisation. It processes key decisions through Board Committees       2015 (‘SEBI Listing Regulations’), and applicable RBI regulations, the Board of Directors (‘Board’) has constituted the
with clearly defined roles and responsibilities. Internal working sub-committees and groups further support these                following Committees.
Committees with cross-functional expertise.
                                                                                                                                 Together, these Committees deliver specialised administration within the overall governance framework,
This framework integrates senior management inputs with formal Board oversight to strengthen decision-making. This               strengthening effectiveness, ensuring clear accountability across defined roles and responsibilities.
approach creates a continuous cycle of informed insight and disciplined decision-making across all levels of the organisation.
As a result, your Company ensures consistency, accountability, and effective governance throughout its operations.

                                                                                                                         Role of the Board
                                                                                                                         The Board provides strategic leadership and ensures strong governance oversight across the organisation. It guides your
                                                                                                                         Company’s long-term direction while aligning decisions with overall business objectives.

                                                                                                                         At the same time, the Board balances growth ambitions with prudent risk management practices. It also ensures regulatory
                                                                                                                         compliance and accountability across all levels.

                                                                                                                         Key responsibilities of the Board include:


                                                                                                                             Strategic Direction
                                                                                                                             Setting your Company’s long-term vision, approving strategic priorities, and reviewing progress against
                                                                                                                             defined objectives




                                                                                                                             Risk and Capital Oversight
                                                                                                                             Overseeing the risk appetite framework, capital discipline, and key financial and non-financial risks to ensure
                                                                                                                             sustainable growth




                                                                                                                             Performance Monitoring
                                                                                                                             Tracking business performance, operational discipline, and management execution against approved plans



Average Tenure of the Board                                      Average Tenure of the Board
                                                                                                                             Governance and Compliance

                                                                                                                             promoting ethical conduct across the organisation

                                                                                                                             Stakeholder Alignment
                                                                                                                             Safeguarding the interests of shareholders and other stakeholders by ensuring that strategy, risk, and conduct

Directors
                                                                                                                             Transparency and Disclosure
                                                                                                                             Ensuring timely, accurate, and transparent disclosures across financial and non-financial matters, including
                                                                                                                             reporting and sustainable value creation


Collectively, the Board’s composition reflects an optimal blend of continuity, experience, diversity and independence,   While specialised Committees support focused checks across key areas, the Board retains collective responsibility for
supporting robust management and long-term value creation.                                                               governance and oversight. This integrated approach ensures that decision-making is not fragmented, but firmly anchored
                                                                                                                         at the Board level, enabling balanced judgement and consistent governance outcomes.

All appointments and reappointments to the Board are conducted in compliance with applicable legal provisions.                    In a constantly shifting business environment, the Board serves as a steady anchor. It guides direction, balancing priorities and
A structured and transparent process is designed to ensure continuity, independence, and the availability of appropriate skills   responds to emerging complexities. At the same time, it remains focused on your Company’s core purpose and long-term
at the Board level.                                                                                                               aspirations. Beyond oversight, the Board actively shapes strategic choices and ensures that your Company remains adaptable,

Board Capability and Expertise
The Board brings together a broad mix of skills aligned with your Company’s operations. It remains committed to upholding high
standards of governance across all aspects of its operations. LTF consistently strives to create sustainable long-term value for all
stakeholders. It ensures comprehensive compliance with applicable laws, rules, and regulations, as well as adoption of best practices.
The governance culture is anchored in accountability, transparency and ethical conduct. These principles guide all strategic and
operational decisions across the organisation. They also strengthen confidence among stakeholders and regulatory bodies. The Board
understands your Company’s operating context and strategic priorities. This understanding enables informed and balanced oversight.
These capabilities are applied in an integrated manner, allowing the Board to assess interlinkages across growth, risk, technology, and
sustainability.

To align the interests of our workforce with long-term value creation, your Company grants Employee Stock Option Plans (ESOPs)
to eligible employees. This initiative fosters a culture of ownership and rewards LTF’s employees' dedication to driving your
Company’s growth. The ESOPs are granted under the ESOPs schemes approved by the shareholders and monitored by the NRC.

Integration of ESG KPIs and Accountability
     KPIs pertaining to sustainability are embedded into Senior Management performance metrics, ensuring leadership
     accountability for sustainability outcomes
     Business heads and function heads work directly with the sustainability team to drive ESG initiatives at the
     operational level
     Dedicated working groups like
        Sustainable Finance Working Group: This dedicated group is responsible for operationalising the
        Sustainable Finance Framework, and maintains rigorous internal governance over product screening and
        manages the green and social tagging processes essential for directing capital toward sustainable development.
      	Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) Council: Comprising of senior business leaders and functional heads, this
        council elevates DEI from a standard initiative to a core business outcome owned by leadership. It provides
        strategic direction and ensures inclusive practices are woven into your company’s operational and management
        accountability.
      	Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) Working Group: A dedicated group ensuring workplace safety
        and regulatory compliance for health and safety practices. This group integrates expertise from facilities
        management and the sustainability team and drives a proactive safety culture to manage system-driven
        frameworks across entire branch network.

Fraud Risk Management
In an era of rapid financial digitisation, at LTF, we recognise that a resilient Fraud Risk Management (FRM) framework is not
merely a regulatory mandate but a fundamental pillar of our enterprise risk strategy. As an Upper Layer NBFC, your Company
has institutionalised a comprehensive FRM framework that is meticulously aligned with the RBI Master Directions. Your
Company's approach is predicated on the belief that safeguarding its financial integrity and the trust of stakeholders requires
a dynamic, "Zero Tolerance" stance against unethical practices.

This commitment is reflected in our multi-layered governance architecture, where accountability is driven from the top. LTF
has a dedicated Risk Containment Unit (RCU) entrusted with the responsibility of Fraud Risk Management and safeguarding
Company against financial frauds. The Senior Management oversees initiatives being taken by this unit through the Fraud
Risk Management Group (FRMG) which serves as the primary body for monitoring effectiveness of FRM framework. The
Special Committee of the Board (SCBMF) and the Audit Committee (ACB) provide rigorous quarterly oversight, scrutinising
root causes and enforcing staff accountability to ensure that the defensive posture remains impenetrable


At LTF, Fraud Risk Management is operationalised through 4 pillars:

Employee Volunteering, Boondein
The Employee Volunteering Program – Boondein has grown into a high-impact initiative. A total of 1,000+ employees,
contributed 2,014 hours to social and environmental causes. Activities included recording audiobooks, creating braille books,
developing teaching materials, upcycling T-shirts into tote bags, and crafting eco-friendly bangles. The program is driven by a
strategic collaboration between HR and CSR teams to foster a culture of giving and community engagement.


Through these initiatives, LTF continues to foster a culture of participation, creativity, wellness, and inclusivity, connecting
employees across locations and building a vibrant, people-first workplace.

Focus                                                                                                                                                          Our Sustainability Journey
LTF is guided by its commitment to responsible growth, embedding E, S and G consideration into its business and
operations, supported by strong governance, robust risk management and a focus on delivering consistent, measurable and              At LTF, sustainability is backed by action, evidence and industry-first commitments. From being amongst
long-term value.                                                                                                                        first to conduct Double Materiality Assessments to measuring business impact in monetary terms,
                                                                                                                                      managing climate risks, achieving voluntary assurance ahead of regulatory mandates, and partnering

                   Vision                                                                                                             for carbon accounting your Company’s sustainability journey is defined by rigour, transparency and a
                                                                                                                                                                         determination to lead from the front.

                   To be an environmentally and socially responsible financial institution built
                   on the foundation of ‘Assurance’, focused on generating sustainable long-
                   term value for all stakeholders.

                                                                                                         Building a Sustainability Strategy Roadmap
           Short-term                                                                                    Your Company has a structured and forward-looking approach for embedding sustainability into its core business strategy.
           Establishing the Foundations                                         OUTCOME                  The approach begins with a maturity and materiality assessment, through which key action areas and material ESG priorities
                                                                                                         are identified, supported by leadership inputs. These insights shape your Company’s ESG purpose, vision, sustainability goals,
                                                                                                         and cross functional defined KPIs, forming the foundation of its sustainability journey. Building on this, your Company's
             Focus                                                                                       sustainability roadmap is aligned with globally recognised standards and frameworks such as those of regulators and
           Governance, structure, compliance, baseline development                                       international bodies, while also benchmarking against leading industry peers. This ensures that your Company’s approach
                                                                                                         remains both compliant and competitive. The roadmap is then fed into an ESG risk-embedded strategy, where sustainability
              Strengthen sustainability governance and oversight                                         is integrated into decision-making and risk management. This enables tangible outcomes including improved sustainable

 IMPACT STUDY

  Business Impact Study                                                                                                   The True Value Bridge
                                                                                                                          A key output of the study is the True Value Bridge, which combines your Company’s reported financial performance with the
  In FY26, your Company conducted its 2nd Business Impact Study with the support                                          monetised economic, social and environmental impacts generated through its activities.

  of an external consultant to assess the broader value created through its business
  activities. The study marks an important milestone in your Company’s journey to
  measure performance beyond financial outcomes and understand how its business
                                                                                                                                                                                            0
  contributes to economic growth, social progress and environmental stewardship.
                                                                                                                                                                                                       ` 3,287Cr
  The assessment applies a True Value methodology, which evaluates how lending,                                                                                                                                                      ` 63,960 Cr
                                                                                                                                                                     ` 18,800 Cr                                     `(6,336 Cr)
  operations and digital innovation create measurable outcomes for customers, communities,
  employees, the environment and the wider economy. By monetising these outcomes, the
  study provides a holistic view of performance and helps quantify your Company’s True                                                ` 48,208 Cr          0

  Earnings – the value created for society alongside financial returns.


  How the Study Was Conducted
  The assessment followed a structured and globally aligned methodology designed to ensure transparency, credibility
  and comparability of results.

Materiality Analysis                                                                                      Double Materiality Assessment framework (DMA)
                                                                                                          LTF’s Double Materiality framework evaluates sustainability through two complementary perspectives:

PRIORITISING
                                                                                                                                                                         Assessment Framework
WHAT MATTERS                                                                                                                                     Impact Materiality                                           Financial Materiality

Your Company’s approach to                The findings of this assessment continue to guide priorities
                                          in FY26, with no material shifts observed in the business
                                                                                                                  How your Company's actions impact people
                                                                                                                            and the environment
                                                                                                                                                                                                     How environmental and social issues affect your

F I N A N C I A L C A P I TA L


DRIVING
                                 At LTF, performance is shaped by a disciplined approach to growth that carefully balances scale, stability, and
                                 efficiency. In a dynamic operating environment, your Company continues to strengthen profitability, enhance
                                 portfolio quality and reinforce balance sheet resilience through prudent financial management, diversified
                                 funding and robust risk governance. This is complemented by a sharp focus on improving operating efficiency,


PERFORMANCE                      optimising cost of funds and driving productivity through technology-led interventions.
                                 At the same time, your Company remains committed to maintaining asset quality and ensuring that growth
                                 is both, calibrated and sustainable. Strong underwriting practices, continuous portfolio monitoring and


WITH DISCIPLINE                  conservative provisioning provide resilience across cycles, while a granular and diversified retail franchise
                                 supports stable and predictable earnings. This integrated approach enables sustained growth, improves returns,
                                 and supports responsible expansion, while remaining firmly aligned with long-term strategic priorities and
                                 stakeholder expectations.

                                                                                                                                    LTF’s ability to deliver consistent performance is anchored in disciplined financial management, robust risk governance, and
                                                                                                                                    prudent funding strategies. It maintains strong capital adequacy and conservative leverage levels, ensuring resilience and the
      International                                                                                                                 capacity to support continued growth. Rigorous underwriting and portfolio monitoring practices enable your Company to


      BBB (Stable)                                         BBB- (Stable)
                                                                                                                                    preserve asset quality and proactively manage risks.
                                                                                                                                    LTF also maintains a diversified funding base, supported by strong domestic and international credit ratings and access to
                                                                                                                                    multiple funding sources, enabling efficient and cost-effective resource mobilisation. Its disciplined asset-liability management

      *PAT before one time labour code effect: ₹3,003 Cr                                                                            remains responsible and aligned with long-term priorities. This balanced and disciplined approach enables LTF to strengthen
                                                                                                                                    its institutional resilience, enhance operational efficiency, and deliver sustained value to stakeholders.

WHY
this matters
A strong and resilient financial foundation is essential to sustaining growth, maintaining stakeholder confidence, and
delivering consistent value across economic cycles. At LTF, financial capital management operates through a disciplined
cycle of mobilising capital from diversified funding sources, deploying it into productive lending opportunities, and recycling
it through repayments and collections to support continued credit expansion. This efficient capital rotation enables your
Company to scale its retail lending franchises while maintaining balance sheet stability, liquidity, and prudent risk discipline.
By maintaining strong capital buffers and disciplined financial management, LTF ensures that funds raised are deployed
responsibly and continuously redeployed to support new lending opportunities. This approach not only strengthens
operational resilience and supports investment in digital capabilities but also advances your Company’s broader purpose of
expanding access to credit, enabling entrepreneurship, and supporting inclusive economic development, while delivering
sustainable, long-term value for stakeholders.

                                                                                                                           Financial Performance and Profitability Momentum
From the CFO’s Desk                                                                                                        FY26 marked a year of exceptional execution and disciplined growth for LTF, reflecting the strength of its diversified retail
                                                                                                                           franchise and sustained focus on building a scalable and technology-led lending platform. Despite operating in a dynamic
                                                                                                                           macroeconomic environment, your Company delivered strong, consistent growth across its lending portfolio while improving
                                                                                                                           profitability, strengthening margins, and enhancing capital efficiency.

                                                                                                                           This performance underscores the resilience of LTF’s business model and its ability to scale sustainably while maintaining

Your Company continued to strengthen its financial position through disciplined capital allocation, prudent provisioning, and         LTF’s continued focus on building a high-quality, granular, and diversified retail portfolio has been central to its
active portfolio monitoring. Provision coverage remained robust at 67%, reinforcing balance sheet resilience and ensuring             transformation into a retail-focused lending franchise. In FY26, your Company successfully strengthened its retail franchise
adequate buffers against potential uncertainties.                                                                                     while maintaining superior asset quality and portfolio resilience.

ensure the long-term sustainability of returns.

M A N U FA C T U R E D A N D I N T E L L E C T U A L C A P I TA L


TRANSFORMING
                                                                    LTF has built a phygital platform-led lending ecosystem that combines AI, advanced analytics, and the PLANET
                                                                    App with a strong nationwide distribution network to deliver at scale and enable inclusive growth. At the core
                                                                    is an integrated digital lending architecture, where proprietary AI engines, Project Cyclops, Project Helios, Project
                                                                    Nostradamus, KAI (Kustomer AI), and Project Orion, power the entire lending lifecycle, from sourcing and


LENDING THROUGH                                                     underwriting to portfolio monitoring, collections, and customer engagement. Together, these engines enable
                                                                    faster decision-making, sharper risk management, and enhanced operational efficiency.
                                                                    Customer and partner engagement is anchored through the PLANET App and Partner PLANET platforms,


DIGITAL INTELLIGENCE                                                enabling seamless, end-to-end digital journeys, real-time servicing, and enhanced transparency, significantly
                                                                    accelerating digital adoption across segments.
                                                                    Together, these capabilities position your Company to scale sustainably, enhance asset quality, improve
                                                                    customer experience, and advance financial inclusion, while delivering measurable impact across key material
                                                                    areas including innovation, compliance, and operational efficiency.

nationwide distribution network.

               Core AI Platforms Powering the Lending Lifecycle                                                                    Runs machine learning models simultaneously

               Project Helios                                                                                                                 Project Nostradamus
               Modernising the Core. Powering the Future.                                                                                     AI-Driven, Real-Time Portfolio Intelligence Engine
               Transforming Credit Decisioning through AI and Unified Underwriting Intelligence                                               Project Nostradamus is LTF’s proprietary, first-in-industry AI-powered automated portfolio management
                                                                                                                                              engine. Designed as the intelligence layer that activates immediately after disbursement, it ensures that risk
               Project Helios is an AI-driven underwriting intelligence platform designed to modernise how credit decisions
                                                                                                                                              oversight does not end at underwriting but continues dynamically across the entire lifecycle of every loan.
               are made across LTF’s lending businesses. As credit evaluation processes became increasingly data-intensive,
               credit managers had to manually analyse multiple reports such as bureau reports, banking statements,                           As the live book scales across products and geographies, Project Nostradamus continuously evaluates
               financial statements, deviation notes, and personal discussions.                                                               millions of active accounts, shifting portfolio management from reactive monitoring to predictive, system-
                                                                                                                                              driven supervision.
               Project Helios addresses this challenge by bringing these fragmented signals together into a single
               intelligent decision support layer.

                Project Orion                                                                                                               KAI
                                                                                                                                            KAI (Kustomer AI) is an Agentic AI-powered conversational assistant designed to transform how
                AI Conversational Co-Pilot for Portfolio Intelligence
                                                                                                                                            customers engage with financial services through natural, human-like interactions. Built on Large
                Project Orion is LTF’s next-generation AI conversational assistant and the interface layer to Project                       Language Model (LLM) technology, KAI enables customers to interact using free-flowing text or voice,
                Nostradamus. While Project Nostradamus generates portfolio-wide predictive insights, Project Orion ensures                  allowing them to query financial products, explore loan options, generate leads, and perform servicing
                that these signals are instantly accessible and actionable for credit and business teams across FY26.                       activities seamlessly.

                                                                                                                                            All AI interactions are unified under the KAI ecosystem. Currently KAI is active on the website chatbot
                                                                                                                                            and voice outreach. We are evolving into a seamless omnichannel presence with KAI expanding into

                                                                                                                                    The system understands all these queries and dynamically retrieves the relevant information or executes
                                                                                                                                    the required service.

Strengthening a Scalable and Secure Technology Backbone                                                                         Data Privacy, Cybersecurity, and Digital Risk Management
As LTF advances its technology-driven retail strategy, strengthening its digital infrastructure and cybersecurity framework     Given the scale of customer data handled across the digital ecosystem, LTF has established a comprehensive data privacy
remains critical to ensuring resilience, scalability, and secure service delivery. Your Company continues to invest in cloud    and cybersecurity framework to safeguard sensitive information, ensure regulatory compliance, and maintain operational
infrastructure, high-availability systems, and robust information security practices to support growing digital volumes while   resilience. Oversight is provided through the Board-level IT Strategy Committee, IT Steering Committee, and Information
safeguarding customer data.                                                                                                     Security Committee, supported by enterprise-wide policies aligned with key regulatory requirements and global standards.

H U M A N C A P I TA L


EMPOWERING TALENT.
                         At LTF, people are central to how the organisation grows, adapts and creates long-term value. This chapter
                         explores how your Company is building a workplace where talent is nurtured, performance is enabled and
                         individuals find meaning in their contributions.



TRANSFORMING THE
                         LTF’s transformation is being driven as much by its people as by its strategy. Growth is not only measured
                         in scale, but also in how individuals evolve and contribute. As the organisation progresses, it is creating an
                         environment where ambition is supported by opportunity, and performance is balanced with care.



FUTURE.
                         The approach reflects multiple dimensions of the employee experience. Teams collaborate with greater agility,
                         leaders enable with intent and everyday moments of support, recognition and learning strengthen a connected
                         workplace. The focus extends beyond programmes and structures to the lived experience of being part of LTF,
                         where trust is built, voices are valued and growth is both personal and shared.
                         This journey is anchored in a clear yet powerful idea: when people have space to grow, confidence to lead and
                         support to succeed, they become the driving force behind sustained performance and meaningful impact.

Role Appreciation Programme (RAP)
The RAP is a structured pre-joining experience designed for frontline officers. It was introduced to address early attrition by
improving role clarity, and ensuring informed commitment prior to onboarding.

Key highlights include:

Blending Field Exposure with                                                          Improving Role Clarity and Informed
Structured Learning                                                                   Decision-Making
Combined immersive on-ground                                                          Provides candidates with a realistic
experience with classroom-based sessions                                              preview of responsibilities, reducing new-
to strengthen readiness and alignment                                                 joiner anxiety and enabling informed
                                                                                      commitment before joining

Operating at Scale across the                                                         Strengthening Early-Stage Retention
Network                                                                               and Stability
 Delivered across 2,200+ branches and                                                 Enhances expectation alignment,
115+ Regional Training Centres, ensuring                                              improves first 30-day retention and
consistency in experience and standards                                               supports frontline workforce stability

                                                                                                                                                                                    Reward Right
talent over the long term. Your Company’s retention approach builds on these pillars, bringing together career progression,
leadership support, rewards, and employee listening into a cohesive framework that sustains engagement and reduces                                                    A high-performance culture thrives when contributions are recognised, performance is rewarded, and employees
attrition.                                                                                                                                                            see a clear link between effort, outcomes and growth. LTF’s rewards philosophy is performance-linked, transparent,
                                                                                                                                                                      and future-focused, combining financial incentives with recognition platforms that celebrate individual and team

driven model. In this structure, workforce feedback, performance data, and leadership accountability come together to shape

S O C I A L A N D R E L AT I O N S H I P C A P I TA L


STRENGTHENING
                                                        LTF’s growth strategy is supported by its consistent focus on customer experience, partner engagement, and
                                                        community development. By integrating digital innovations, responsible practices, and inclusive initiatives, your
                                                        Company is building a connected ecosystem that enhances transparency, improves access, and delivers long-



TRUST, EXPANDING
                                                        term value for all stakeholders. Your Company's CSR initiatives are designed to drive inclusive and sustainable
                                                        development. The programmes focus on digital and financial inclusion, climate impact management, disaster
                                                        management, and social inclusion. Across its value chain, your Company also builds strong partnerships with
                                                        dealers and vendors. These relationships are anchored in sustainability aligned practices.

CSR Governance
A strong governance framework forms the backbone of our CSR efforts, ensuring accountability, compliance, and strategic
alignment. The Board of Directors and CSR & Sustainability Committee oversee the planning, execution, and monitoring of
all initiatives, ensuring they align with organisational objectives and regulatory requirements.

                                                                                                                                                                                       multiple-cropping cycles.
                                                                                                                                                                                       Crop Diversification
                                                                                                                                                                                       The shift has enabled farmers to cultivate high-value cash
                                                                                                                                                                                       crops and vegetables during the rabi season, boosting farm
                                                                                                                                                                                       income and reducing the need for seasonal migration.
                                                                                                                                                                                       Community-Led Sustainability
                                                                                                                                                                                       Established Water User Groups (WUGs) at each site to
                                                                                                                                                                                       ensure local ownership. With dedicated bank accounts and
                                                                                                                                                                                       an initial maintenance grant of ₹10,000 per group, the
                                                                                                                                                                                       community is empowered to manage operational costs and
                                                                                                                                                                                       repairs through collective fee collection, ensuring the long-
                                                                                                                                                                                       term functionality of the solar infrastructure.

                                                                                                                  Through these interventions, more reliable access to water is being created for both agricultural and domestic needs, directly
                                                                                                                  supporting productivity and livelihood stability.

Strengthening CSR through Third-Party
Stakeholder Engagement
In a first-of-its-kind initiative in the BFSI sector, we partnered with CRISIL to undertake a comprehensive third-party
stakeholder engagement study, reinforcing our commitment to transparency, inclusivity, and impact-driven CSR. This
exercise went beyond conventional assessments by systematically capturing insights from all key stakeholders, community
members, Digital Sakhis, implementation partners, local institutions, and internal teams.

The study enabled us to understand on-ground realities, expectations, and gaps across programme geographies. By
incorporating these diverse perspectives, we ensured that every stakeholder became an active participant in shaping the
CSR interventions, rather than just a beneficiary.

It also helped align interventions more closely with community needs, ensuring higher adoption, ownership, and long-term
sustainability.

This collaborative and evidence-led approach has significantly improved the effectiveness of programme execution on the
ground. By embedding stakeholder voices into decision-making, we have built a more responsive and resilient CSR model
setting a new benchmark for the BFSI sector in driving inclusive and participatory development.

N AT U R A L C A P I TA L


PROGRESSING
                            As LTF expands its footprint across geographies and business segments, your Company recognises that growth
                            must be accompanied by stronger environmental responsibility. With increasing scale, embedding sustainability
                            into core operations and decision-making has become essential.



ENVIRONMENTAL
                            The focus now extends beyond tracking performance to building robust systems that enhance accountability,
                            enable better data utilisation, and support proactive risk management. Efforts to optimise energy use, manage
                            emissions, and improve resource efficiency particularly water and waste are being strengthened across
                            operations.


STEWARDSHIP                 Alongside this, your Company continues to drive on-ground impact through energy-efficiency initiatives,
                            resource optimisation, renewable energy adoption, community-led water initiatives, and plantation programmes.
                            These efforts reflect a balanced approach that integrates strategic intent with practical action.

       Scope 2 Emissions                                                                                                                                  Scope 3 Emissions

       Indirect Emissions from Energy Consumption                                                                                   I    II III           Other Indirect GHG Emissions                                                                               I II III
Scope 2 emissions represent indirect emissions associated with purchased electricity consumed across offices, and                                      Scope 3 emissions encompass indirect activities across your Company’s broader value chain, including capital goods
branches, and data centres. Scope 2 emissions reflect electricity consumption across offices, branches, and data centres                               procurement, employee commuting, business travel, and financed emissions linked to the lending portfolio. Recognizing
and are influenced by the energy management initiatives described earlier in this chapter.                                                             that these represent a significant portion of the total environmental impact, your Company initiated strategic measures in
Your Company has actively accelerated its transition to clean energy, successfully meeting 100% of the electricity                                     FY26 to mitigate these emissions.
requirements for its Head Office and 61 branches through green power. To ensure comprehensive accountability, your                                     LTF measures and monitors relevant Scope 3 categories in line with the GHG Protocol, prioritising those most relevant to
Company adopts both location-based and market-based approaches for calculating Scope 2 emissions. While the location-                                  its business model.
based method reflects grid consumption at operational sites, the market-based method highlights the positive impact of
renewable energy procurement, providing a transparent and nuanced view of your Company’s carbon footprint in line                                      Key categories assessed include:
with global best practices.

Recognizing the operational constraints of small, leased branches without dedicated metering, where direct renewable
                                                                                                                                                            Purchased Goods                       Capital                    Fuel and Energy               Waste generated in
procurement is not feasible, your Company bridged this gap by leveraging International Renewable Energy Certificates
                                                                                                                                                              and Services                        Goods                      related activities               operations
(iRECs). These certificates, sourced from verified Indian projects and compliant with RE100 criteria, allow your Company to
claim green attributes for its total electricity consumption.

Beyond procurement, your Company has invested in on-site generation through solar rooftop PV projects at six rural
branches. Complementing these energy transitions are significant infrastructure upgrades, including the deployment of                                                          Business                        Employee                    Upstream Leased
IoT devices, LED lighting, and 5-star rated ACs. Collectively, these initiatives led to a ~8% tCO2e reduction in Scope 2
                                                                                                                                                                                Travel                        Commuting                         Assets
emissions. Notably, without your Company's dedicated efforts to secure green power and iRECs, Scope 2 emissions would
have reached ~7,335 tCO2e under the location-based assessment.

Waste Management: Moving from Disposal to Discipline                                                                                                  Embedding Better Waste Practices
While LTF’s business model does not generate manufacturing or product waste, it remains focused on reducing operational
waste, improving segregation, and strengthening recycling outcomes across its footprint.

During the year, your Company prioritised better measurement before reduction.

    Conduct Hazard Identification & Risk Assessment (HIRA)                                                                              carbon neutrality by FY35. LTF has successfully maintained water-positive status through sustained conservation efforts

19.                                                                    Annual value chain meeting conducted                           	The Company has been re-certified as a Great Place To Work® for a second consecutive year. The score improved from 82

*We have over 14,000+ distribution touch points through which we distribute our financial services products wherein customers who come
to purchase farm equipment or two-wheeler may choose our services. These distribution touch points includes authorised dealers of farm and

     Principle                                                                                                                       WASTE AND (D) OTHER WASTE:
                  Businesses should provide goods and services in a manner that is sustainable and safe.
        2                                                                                                                        	
                                                                                                                                  LTF does not manufacture any product that needs to be reclaimed for reusing, recycling and disposing at the end of life. The
                                                                                                                                  Company has taken up and achieved the target of Zero-Single Use plastic at its corporate office and uses steel bins instead
                                                                                                                                  of plastic bins for disposal of waste generated. Through awareness on recycling and segregation of waste, LTF encourages
                                                                                                                                  sustainable ways of handling its waste and has appointed a certified waste segregator at Head Office as part of its action
     Essential Indicators                                                                                                         towards zero waste to landfill.

                                                                                                                                          Employees can report any breach of Code of Conduct by sending an email to code@ltfs.com. The Code of Conduct committee
                                                                                                                                          comprises of senior Company officials, who investigate the complaints thoroughly.

                                                                                                                                          of senior Company officials/leaders, conducts appropriate investigation into the complaints.

2.	
   PROVIDE THE MEASURES UNDERTAKEN BY THE ENTITY TO ENSURE THAT STATUTORY DUES HAVE BEEN
   DEDUCTED AND DEPOSITED BY THE VALUE CHAIN PARTNERS:
	LTF has implemented a Third-party Code of Conduct since FY22. The code establishes standards and sets expectations from
  its value chain partners with regards to terms of ethical and responsible business practices and conduct, including interalia
  statutory compliance, governance, sustainable sourcing, environmental stewardship and socially sustainable practices.
  Thus, through awareness and necessary certifications, LTF ensures that statutory dues have been deducted and deposited
  by the value chain partners.

2.    DETAILS OF THE SCOPE AND COVERAGE OF ANY HUMAN RIGHTS DUE-DILIGENCE CONDUCTED
	For promoting human rights and ethical labour practices, the Company has implemented formal policies. To strengthen
  human rights awareness across value Chain Partners, LTF hosted its annual ESG value chain Connect in December 2025,
  engaging partners specifically on human rights, and responsible business conduct.
	Internally, human rights are governed continuously through the Company's Human Rights Policy, Code of Conduct, and
  structured grievance mechanisms which ensure a zero-tolerance approach to discrimination, child labour, forced labour,
  and harassment.

3.	IS THE PREMISE/OFFICE OF THE ENTITY ACCESSIBLE TO DIFFERENTLY ABLED VISITORS, AS PER THE REQUIREMENTS
    OF THE RIGHTS OF PERSONS WITH DISABILITIES ACT, 2016?
	LTF has successfully achieved 100% accessibility across its large offices and major branches (15,000 sq. ft. and above).
  The Company has adopted a formal target to further improve workplace accessibility for (PwDs) across additional branch
  offices by FY28. A Liaison Officer has been appointed to oversee and manage the recruitment and inclusion of PwDs. This
  commitment is formally documented and enforced through the Company’s Human Rights Policy and Diversity, Equity and
  Inclusion (DEI) Policy.

	As a financial services institution with office-based operations, LTF does not generate industrial effluents. The Company's        FORMAT:
  wastewater primarily consists of domestic sewage. To manage this responsibly and enhance water recycling, LTF successfully     	LTF operates as an NBFC with a service-oriented model and no manufacturing activities. Consequently, there are no

5.	DOES THE ENTITY HAVE A BUSINESS CONTINUITY AND DISASTER MANAGEMENT PLAN? GIVE DETAILS IN 100
    WORDS/WEB LINK.
                                                                                                                                         Principle       Businesses, when engaging in influencing public and regulatory policy, should do so in a
	LTF maintains a comprehensive Business Continuity Policy (Non-IT) framework to encompass operational and human capital
                                                                                                                                                         manner that is responsible and transparent
  continuity. The Board's Risk Management Committee actively oversees and reviews the business continuity plans to ensure                   7
  enterprise resilience.

	The Business Continuity Plan (BCP) is a critical component of LTF’s risk management framework. It is designed to ensure
  the continuity of essential business functions and services in the face of unforeseen disruptions. The primary objective               Essential Indicators
  of this plan is to provide a structured, actionable framework to respond effectively to an incident, minimize downtime,
  protect critical assets and facilitate a rapid and organized recovery. By defining key roles, responsibilities, and procedures,   1.     a.     Number of affiliations with trade and industry chambers/associations.
  this document serves as a guide for maintaining operational stability and mitigating the impact of any interruption on our        		LTF's active memberships, leadership roles, and affiliations with seven (7) key trade, industry, and sustainability
  stakeholders, partners, and customers. The framework includes forward-looking scenario analysis, periodic testing under              chambers/associations.
  multiple adverse scenarios.

 Independent Assurance Statement on Business Responsibility and Sustainability Report (BRSR) disclosures and Integrated

 Introduction and objective of engagement                                                                                                  There are inherent limitations in assurance engagement, including, for example, the use of judgment and selective testing of

 BDO’s responsibility                                                                                                                      We have reviewed the disclosures in the “Report” for the reporting period from 1st April 2025 through 31st March 2026. The

 those of IR as described in the ‘Scope & boundary of assurance’ section below. We do not accept or assume any responsibility for          Our conclusions

We further report that
The Board of Directors of the Company is duly constituted with proper balance of Executive Directors,
Non-Executive Directors and Independent Directors. The change in the composition of the Board of Directors that
took place during the period under review were carried out in compliance with the provisions of the Act.
Adequate notice is given to all directors to schedule the Board Meetings, agenda and detailed notes on agenda
were sent at least seven days in advance, and a system exists for seeking and obtaining further information and
clarifications on the agenda items before the meeting and for a meaningful participation at the meeting.
The minutes of the Board Meetings and Committee Meetings have not identified any dissent by members of the
Board/Committee of the Board; hence we have no reason to believe that the decisions by the Board were not
approved by all the directors present. The Minutes of the Board Meetings and Committee Meetings were duly
approved at the meeting by the Chairperson of the Meeting.
We further report that there are adequate systems and processes in the Company commensurate with its
size and operations, to monitor and ensure compliance with applicable laws, rules, regulations and guidelines.
As informed, the Company has responded appropriately to communication received from various statutory /
regulatory authorities including initiating actions for corrective measures, wherever found necessary.
We further report that during the audit period the following events/actions have taken place, having a major
bearing on the Company’s affairs in pursuance of the above referred laws, rules, regulations, guidelines and
standards:
1.   Approval of the Shareholders of the Company was obtained at the Annual General Meeting of the Company
     held on June 3, 2025 for alteration of the Articles of Association of the Company.
2.   During the financial year, the Company has issued and allotted 95,17,915 Equity Shares of Rs.10 each under
     the L&T Finance Limited Employee Stock Option Scheme – 2013.
3.   During the financial year, the Company has allotted 7,47,500 Senior, Secured, Rated, Listed, Redeemable,
     Non-Convertible Debentures of R 1,00,000/- each for cash at par on a private placement basis.

48.8 Disclosure pursuant to Ind AS 107 “Financial Instruments: Disclosures“                                         		      The Company’s objectives when managing capital are to					
    Liquidity risk management :                                                                                     		      (a) 	safeguard their ability to continue as a going concern, so that they can continue to provide returns
                                                                                                                                  for shareholders and benefits for other stakeholders, and
 (a) Maturity profile of financial liabilities based on undiscounted cashflows
										                                                                                    (₹ in crore)          		      (b) Maintain an optimal capital structure to reduce the cost of capital.
                                            As at March 31, 2026             As at March 31, 2025                   		      (c) 	The Company’s assessment of capital requirement is aligned to its planned growth which forms part
                                      Within       After                Within      After                                         of an annual operating plan which is approved by the Board and also a long range strategy. These
     Particulars
                                      twelve      twelve       Total    twelve     twelve       Total                             growth plans are aligned to assessment of risks - which include credit, liquidity and market.
                                      month       month                 month      month
                                                                                                                    		In order to maintain or adjust the capital structure, the Company may adjust the amount of dividends paid
     A. Non-derivative liabilities:
                                                                                                                       to shareholders, return capital to shareholders, issue new shares or sell assets to reduce debt.

48.9 Capital management						                                                                                                                                                             March 31, 2026            March 31, 2025

    (i) Risk management						
                                                                                                                            Assets – Cross Currency Interest Rate Swap Contracts        USD 86,77,79,999.97         USD 88,16,67,222
		As an NBFC, the RBI requires us to maintain a minimum capital to risk weighted assets ratio (“CRAR”)
   consisting of Tier I and Tier II capital of 15% of our aggregate risk weighted assets out of which Tier I
   Capital should be at least 9% of risk weighted assets. Further, the total of our Tier II capital cannot exceed
   100% of our Tier I capital at any point of time. The capital management process of the Company ensures
   to maintain a healthy CRAR at all the times. Refer note 52.1 for the Company’s Capital ratios.

		* excludes assets originated or purchased and derecognised during the year                                                                        The estimation of the risk of credit exposures is complex, as the same varies with changes in market conditions,
                                                                                                                                                    expected cash flow and the passage of time. Wholesale and retail portfolios are managed separately to reflect
46 Risk Management:
                                                                                                                                                    the differing nature of the business strategy. As the Company is completely exiting the wholesale business by
    Basis                                                                                                                                           way of sell down, the wholesale portfolio is classified as Fair Value through Profit and Loss Account (“FVTPL”)
                                                                                                                                                    and valued accordingly as per Ind AS 109. As regards the retail portfolio, the same is classified as amortized
    Robust risk management involves a systematic approach to identification, measurement and control of
                                                                                                                                                    cost as per Ind AS 109 and assessed accordingly. The assessment of credit risk of the retail portfolio entails
    various risks. All employees of the Group are responsible for the management of risks, including the Board of
                                                                                                                                                    estimations as to the likelihood of defaults occurring and of the associated loss ratios. The Company measures
    Directors. The Board of Directors and its Risk Management Committee ensure that Management takes into
                                                                                                                                                    credit risk for each class of loan assets using inputs such as ¬Probability of Default (PD) and Loss Given Default
    consideration all the relevant risk factors which could lead to unexpected fluctuations in financials or loss of
                                                                                                                                                    (LGD). PD and LGD are ascertained as per applicable standards culminating in Expected Credit Loss (“ECL”).
    capital employed. Risks are evaluated from time to time and control measures as per defined frameworks as
    approved by the board are executed. This helps in aligning the risk appetite to the Group’s strategy to deliver                                 Retail Business- (Rural and Urban Finance)
    sustainable, long-term returns to its investors.
                                                                                                                                                    A combination of credit models along with policy rules are deployed as approved by the designated officials
                                                                                                                                                    for the respective product. The rules are regularly monitored and updated to ensure that the learnings from
                                                                                                                                                    the portfolio performance and changes in the economic environment are factored in to strengthen the credit
                                                                                                                                                    portfolio..

                                                                                                                         The Board of Directors at its Meeting held on April 24, 2026 has approved the aforementioned amendment to the
                                                                                                                         MOA of the Company, subject to approval of the Members of the Company.
                                                                                                                         A copy of the proposed amended MOA is available for inspection as specified in the Notice.
                                                                                                                         As per the provisions of section 13 of the Companies Act, 2013 read with relevant rules made thereunder, a
                                                                                                                         Company can alter the provisions of its memorandum, in regards to the aforesaid, subject to approval of the
                                                                                                                         Members by the way of an ordinary resolution.
                                                                                                                         The Board is of the opinion that the aforementioned change in the MOA will enable the Company to raise funds
                                                                                                                         through Preference Shares and is in the best interest of the Company and accordingly recommends the ordinary
                                                                                                                         resolution set forth in Item No. 4 of the Notice for approval of the Members.
                                                                                                                         None of the Directors or Key Managerial Personnel of the Company including their relatives are interested or
                                                                                                                         concerned in the Resolution except to the extent of their shareholding, if any, in the Company.
